critic_raw: A sepsis model validated at one external hospital does not provide adequate evidence for clinical deployment. Single-site external validation, even with the same EHR vendor, does not establish generalizability. Subgroup estimates with wide confidence intervals are uninformative at best and potentially misleading at worst. The local intercept recalibration step adds a degree of freedom that could inflate apparent calibration without the model being genuinely portable.
defender_raw: The study explicitly restricts the claim to hospitals matching the EHR configuration and workflow described. Single-site external validation can be adequate when the claim is explicitly limited to that matched configuration. The recalibration was pre-specified — intercept recalibration using unlabeled prevalence from the first month — and was part of the prospective protocol. Subgroup estimates are disclosed as descriptive only. Both discrimination and calibration targets were pre-specified and met on held-out data. The limitation is a scope restriction, not a methodological flaw.

debate_rounds.critic: The pre-specification of the recalibration step is the critical point that addresses my concern about flexible model fitting to appear portable. If the intercept recalibration was defined before any test data was used, the recalibration cannot be a post-hoc artifact. I concede that concern. My residual point is that the scope restriction needs to be visible and enforced — if the model is deployed beyond the restricted scope based on this evidence, the limitation is being ignored rather than respected. But that is a deployment governance concern, not a methodological flaw in the evaluation itself.
debate_rounds.defender: Agreed. The governance concern is real and important for deployment decisions, but the study's responsibility is to accurately characterize what the evidence supports. The report does this by restricting the claim and explicitly stating other hospital types need separate validation. What happens when governance fails is beyond the scope of the evaluation methodology. The evaluation is sound.

adjudication_raw: After one round, the critic concedes the recalibration concern and acknowledges that the residual deployment-governance concern is outside the scope of evaluation methodology. Defense wins.
all_issues_raised: single-site concern — resolved by limited scope claim and pre-specified recalibration
deployment governance concern — acknowledged as outside evaluation methodology scope

empirical_test.condition: Confirm pre-specified recalibration procedure and deployment restriction are documented before test evaluation; verify held-out partner-hospital cohort meets pre-specified discrimination and calibration targets after recalibration
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Recalibration was applied after inspection of held-out outcomes; or the recalibrated model fails pre-specified targets on the partner-site holdout
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Pre-registration restricts claim to matched EHR configuration; intercept recalibration is pre-specified; held-out cohort meets both targets
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Overall performance meets targets but one subgroup shows unstable calibration with wide intervals not clearly flagged as descriptive
